name: Henri Woode
description: American composer, lyricist, arranger, and singer (1909-1994)
abstract: William Henri Woode (September 25, 1909 – May 31, 1994) was an American composer, lyricist, arranger, pianist and singer. His compositions include A Night at the Vanguard, Sweet Slumber, You Taught Me to Love Again, and the jazz standard Broadway popularized by the Count Basie Orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Samuel Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Henri Woode, There was a composer by the name of Henry Woods... [INTERVIEWEE] Was he a Kansas City musician?

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: then after that, Henri Woode would take over, but the first show was conducted by Tom Whaley.
